content.description:
Base type for executable items. Any item with `kernel:code` in its type chain is a code item—its `content.code` field contains JavaScript that gets evaluated as an ES module.

## How Code Execution Works

When a code item is loaded via `api.require()`, the module system evaluates it:

![](item://33333333-4444-0000-0000-000000000000?symbol=evaluateCodeItem) 

The `//# sourceURL` comment gives meaningful names in stack traces and the debugger.

## The `content.code` Field

Must be valid ES module JavaScript.

## Caching

Modules are cached by item ID with their `modified` timestamp. When you save a code item:
1. The `modified` timestamp updates
2. Next `api.require()` detects the timestamp change
3. Module is re-evaluated from fresh code

This enables live editing—change code, save, and new behavior takes effect.

## Loading Code Items

```javascript
// By name (searches code items)
const lib = await api.require('my-library');

// By UUID (direct lookup)
const lib = await api.require('66666666-1234-5678-...');
```

## Code Item Subtypes

| Type | Purpose | Key Export |
|------|---------|------------|
| [kernel:library](item://66666666-0000-0000-0000-000000000000) | Reusable utilities | Any exports |
| [kernel:view](item://aaaaaaaa-0000-0000-0000-000000000000) | Renders items | `render(item, api)` |
| kernel:kernel-module | Core system code | Various |

## Error Handling

If code fails to parse or throws during evaluation:
- Error is caught by the module system
- Stack trace includes item name via sourceURL
- System continues running (errors don't crash Hobson)
- Error items created if [error handler](item://e7707000-0000-0000-0000-000000000002) is active

## See Also

- [kernel:library](item://66666666-0000-0000-0000-000000000000) — Reusable code modules
- [kernel:view](item://aaaaaaaa-0000-0000-0000-000000000000) — Code that renders items
- [kernel:module-system](item://33333333-4444-0000-0000-000000000000) — The code loading infrastructure
